components.name: Media Player
components.description: Removes the modern Media Player app, which still ships under the legacy ZuneMusic package name. Absent from N editions.
breaks: Audio and video files have no default player until you install one
Double-clicking an MP3 or MP4 opens the "how do you want to open this" picker with nothing in it

components.name: Photos
breaks: Image files have no default viewer — Windows Photo Viewer is still on the image but is not registered for any file type by default
Thumbnail previews in File Explorer still work, but there is nothing to open a picture in

components.name: Camera
breaks: No way to take a photo or test a webcam without third-party software
Windows Hello face enrolment still works; it does not use this app

components.name: Sound Recorder
components.description: Removes the Sound Recorder app. Absent from N editions.

components.name: Solitaire Collection
components.description: Removes the Microsoft Solitaire Collection, the largest single casual app on the image.

components.name: Paint
components.description: Removes Paint. It is an appx on this build family, not the capability it used to be.
breaks: The Edit option on images in File Explorer and the Snipping Tool disappears
Nothing on the image can crop or annotate a screenshot afterwards

components.name: Snipping Tool
components.description: Removes the Snipping Tool, which also owns the Print Screen and Win+Shift+S capture shortcuts.
breaks: Win+Shift+S and the Print Screen key stop opening the capture overlay and do nothing at all
Screen recording and the built-in text extraction from screenshots are gone
Print Screen still copies the whole screen to the clipboard, which is the only capture left

components.name: Alarms, To Do & Feedback extras
components.description: Removes the small in-box utilities that most users never open: Clock/Alarms and Microsoft To Do.

components.name: Media Codec Extensions
components.description: Removes the HEVC, AV1, VP9, MPEG-2, WebP, HEIF and RAW codec extensions. Core preset only. Absent from N editions, which never ship them.
breaks: HEIC photos from any iPhone will not open or generate thumbnails anywhere in Windows
HEVC and AV1 video fails to play in every app that relies on the system decoder, including browsers falling back to hardware decode
RAW files from most cameras lose Explorer thumbnails and previews
Some video editors and streaming apps refuse to launch when the system codec is missing rather than falling back to software
